title: LLM Jailbreak
abstract: Adversaries use carefully crafted prompts to bypass LLM safety controls and guardrails, forcing the model to generate unrestricted or unsafe responses.
case_studies: AML.CS0041 - Rules File Backdoor: Supply Chain Attack on AI Coding Assistants (Pillar Security)
AML.CS0046 - Data Destruction via Indirect Prompt Injection Targeting Claude Computer-Use (HiddenLayer)

title: LLM Prompt Injection
abstract: Adversaries craft malicious prompts that cause an LLM to ignore original instructions and follow attacker-controlled behavior, enabling unintended or privileged actions.
case_studies: AML.CS0037 - Data Exfiltration via Agent Tools in Copilot Studio (Zenity)
mitigations: AML.M0019 - Control Access to AI Models and Data in Production
AML.M0033 - Input and Output Validation for AI Agent Components

title: RAG Poisoning
abstract: Adversaries inject malicious or misleading content into documents indexed by a RAG system so poisoned content appears in future retrieval results.
case_studies: AML.CS0026 - Financial Transaction Hijacking with M365 Copilot (Zenity)
AML.CS0035 - Data Exfiltration from Slack AI via Indirect Prompt Injection (PromptArmor)

title: False RAG Entry Injection
abstract: Adversaries introduce false documents into a RAG database so that when retrieved, the LLM treats malicious content as a legitimate RAG result.
case_studies: AML.CS0026 - Financial Transaction Hijacking with M365 Copilot (Zenity)

title: Training Data Poisoning
abstract: Adversaries manipulate training data to influence model behavior and cause incorrect or malicious outputs.

title: AI Model Extraction
abstract: Adversaries attempt to extract or replicate the functionality of a deployed AI model by repeatedly querying it and analyzing the outputs.
mitigations: AML.M0019 - Control Access to AI Models and Data in Production

title: Model Evasion
abstract: Adversaries craft inputs designed to evade AI-based detection and bypass classification or security controls.

title: AI Artifact Collection
abstract: Adversaries collect artifacts related to AI systems such as models, training data, prompts, or configuration files.
mitigations: AML.M0019 - Control Access to AI Models and Data in Production

title: Model Backdoor
abstract: Adversaries embed hidden triggers or backdoors in AI models so that specific inputs activate malicious behavior.

title: Adversarial Examples
abstract: Adversaries craft carefully modified inputs that cause AI models to produce incorrect predictions or classifications.

title: Model Inversion
abstract: Adversaries attempt to reconstruct sensitive training information by analyzing a model’s outputs.
mitigations: AML.M0019 - Control Access to AI Models and Data in Production

title: AI Supply Chain Compromise
abstract: Adversaries compromise AI supply chain components such as models, datasets, or libraries to introduce malicious behavior.
